hero.headline: Galgotias University Online MBA in Operations Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved, NAAC A+, WES recognised. Total all-in cost Rs 86,400 across 2 years, the second-lowest MBA fee on EdifyEdu after IGNOU. Operations Management specialisation with Sourcing and Materials Management, SCM and Logistics Management, Total Quality Management, Project Management, and Management of Service Operations in Sem 3-4. No EMI, no scholarships, no bundled perks.

sections.tldr: Galgotias Online MBA Operations Management: NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, WES recognised, Rs 86,400 all-in (second-lowest after IGNOU Rs 66,000). Sem 3-4 covers Sourcing and Materials Management, SCM and Logistics Management, Total Quality Management, Project Management, and Management of Service Operations. No EMI, no scholarships, no perks. Best fit for budget-first operations professionals who want a legitimate NAAC A+ MBA at under Rs 1 lakh.

about.body:
Galgotias University, Greater Noida, was established in 2011 and holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval for its online programmes. The university is WES recognised for international credential evaluation.

The Online MBA in Operations Management covers core MBA subjects in Sem 1-2 and adds operations depth in Sem 3-4 with Sourcing and Materials Management, SCM and Logistics Management, Total Quality Management, Project Management, and Management of Service Operations. Strategic Management and Business Communication round out the final-year curriculum.

At Rs 86,400 all-in, Galgotias is the second-cheapest MBA on EdifyEdu after IGNOU (Rs 66,000). The trade-off: no EMI, no scholarships, no Six Sigma or PMP bundled certification, no Coursera access, no placement support, and limited brand recognition for operations roles at large manufacturers compared to NIRF-ranked competitors. For working professionals in manufacturing, logistics, or service operations who need a formal MBA credential at the lowest possible cost, Galgotias Operations Management is a budget-first option.

sem3Subjects.name: Sourcing and Materials Management
sem3Subjects.description: Cover procurement strategy, vendor development, materials requirement planning (MRP), inventory control models (EOQ, JIT), and purchase contract management.

sem3Subjects.name: SCM and Logistics Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design supply chain networks, manage logistics operations, apply demand forecasting models, and optimise warehouse and transportation systems.

sem3Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply TQM principles, Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, Kaizen, 5S, and statistical process control (SPC) for manufacturing and service quality improvement.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Communication
sem3Subjects.description: Develop written and verbal communication for corporate settings. Covers report writing, presentation skills, and business correspondence.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse competitive environments using Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, and balanced scorecard frameworks. Build corporate and business-level strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Master Thesis (Initial Phase)
sem3Subjects.description: Define research problem, conduct literature review, and finalise methodology for the MBA thesis project.

sem4Subjects.name: Project Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply project planning frameworks (WBS, Gantt, CPM/PERT), resource allocation, risk management, and stakeholder communication for operations projects.

sem4Subjects.name: Management of Service Operations
sem4Subjects.description: Design service delivery systems, manage service capacity, apply queuing theory, and optimise service processes for banking, healthcare, hospitality, and IT services.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Ethics and CSR
sem4Subjects.description: Examine ethical decision-making frameworks, corporate governance codes, CSR reporting standards, and stakeholder management.

sem4Subjects.name: Master Thesis (Final Phase)
sem4Subjects.description: Complete data analysis, write findings, and defend the MBA thesis before the evaluation panel.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 core subjects are common across all Galgotias Online MBA specialisations. See the main Galgotias Online MBA page for the core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Operations Management MBA graduates target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing (automotive, FMCG, pharmaceuticals), Logistics and warehousing, E-commerce fulfilment, IT services delivery, Retail supply chain, Construction and infrastructure

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Production Planning Associate, Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Coordinator, Quality Assurance Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Operations Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Production Manager, Quality Manager, Logistics Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of Operations, VP Supply Chain, Plant Director, Chief Operations Officer, VP Manufacturing

**Typical hiring organisations:** Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, Hero MotoCorp, Hindustan Unilever (supply chain), ITC, Nestle, Amazon India (fulfilment), Flipkart, Delhivery, Blue Dart, L&T, Grasim Industries

Note: Galgotias does not offer dedicated placement support, Six Sigma certification, or corporate tie-ups for online MBA students. Career advancement depends on your existing network and self-sourced opportunities.

skills.body:
The Galgotias Operations Management specialisation builds these skills across Sem 3-4:

**Technical skills:**
- SCM and Logistics Management: supply chain network design, demand forecasting, warehouse optimisation, transportation planning, and logistics cost analysis
- Total Quality Management: Six Sigma DMAIC, SPC chart interpretation, Kaizen event facilitation, 5S implementation, and root cause analysis
- Sourcing and Materials Management: procurement strategy design, vendor evaluation, MRP calculation, EOQ and JIT inventory management, and purchase contract negotiation

**Applied skills:**
- Project Management: WBS creation, Gantt chart scheduling, CPM/PERT analysis, project risk assessment, and stakeholder communication planning
- Management of Service Operations: service capacity planning, queuing theory application, service blueprinting, and process optimisation for IT, banking, and healthcare services

**Soft skills:** Business communication for operations reporting, strategic management for capacity planning decisions, ethical reasoning for supply chain sustainability and labour compliance.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Galgotias Online MBA Operations Management against peers on EdifyEdu:

**vs IGNOU MBA (Rs 66,000):** IGNOU is Rs 20,400 cheaper but does not offer a dedicated Operations Management specialisation with subjects like SCM and Logistics or TQM. Galgotias provides more operations-specific curriculum depth.

**vs Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is 2.6x the cost with NIRF #22 University ranking and a broader operations curriculum. Amity brand carries stronger weight for manufacturing and supply chain hiring. Galgotias wins only on cost.

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NIRF #19 and offers PwC co-teaching modules. CU costs nearly 2x Galgotias. For operations professionals who value institutional ranking and PwC certification, CU justifies the premium.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,61,000-2,00,000):** LPU offers zero-cost EMI, Coursera access, and NIRF #31 ranking with stronger career services. Galgotias at Rs 86,400 is roughly half the price but lacks EMI, Coursera, and placement support.

**vs JIIT Online MBA (Rs 1,82,000):** JIIT offers mandatory internship and AI-core modules that are increasingly relevant for smart manufacturing and Industry 4.0 roles. JIIT costs over 2x Galgotias. For tech-driven operations roles, JIIT provides stronger practical exposure.

fees.body:
Galgotias University Online MBA in Operations Management has a single fee track with no EMI, no scholarships, and no bundled perks.

**Total all-in cost: Rs 86,400**

- Tuition fee: Rs 76,200 (Rs 33,000/year approx, paid per semester)
- Examination fee: Rs 8,000 (Rs 4,000/year)
- Registration fee: Rs 1,200 (one-time)
- Alumni fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)

**No EMI facility** from the university. No third-party EMI partner announced.
**No scholarships** or fee waivers available.
**No bundled perks** such as Six Sigma certification, Coursera, or placement guarantees.

This is the second-lowest MBA fee on EdifyEdu after IGNOU at Rs 66,000. The fee advantage is the primary reason to consider Galgotias over NIRF-ranked competitors.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Galgotias each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Galgotias University, Greater Noida. It carries the MBA designation with Operations Management specialisation noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is mentioned per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The degree is WES recognised for international credential evaluation. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Galgotias Online MBA Operations Management students. Ratings from post-completion feedback.

items.name: Rahul
items.liked: SCM and Logistics Management was directly relevant to my role at a logistics company. At Rs 86,400, this was the cheapest NAAC A+ operations MBA I found.
items.disliked: No Six Sigma or PMP certification bundled. No career services or placement partnerships.
items.body: I have 6 years in warehouse operations and needed an MBA for promotion to regional operations manager. Galgotias gave me the credential without a loan. The SCM and TQM subjects were practical. Brand recognition at large manufacturers is limited, but for logistics and mid-size manufacturing firms in North India, the NAAC A+ credential is accepted.

items.name: Meena
items.liked: Total Quality Management covered Six Sigma DMAIC in good detail. Project Management was practical with real scheduling examples. The fee advantage is genuine.
items.disliked: No hands-on simulation tools. No factory visits or operations labs. Brand recognition outside UP is weak for operations roles at large manufacturers.
items.body: Good value for the price. If you work in manufacturing in UP, Delhi-NCR, or Rajasthan and need an MBA credential under Rs 1 lakh, Galgotias Operations Management works. For operations careers at top auto or FMCG manufacturers, LPU or Amity will carry more weight.

items.name: Kunal
items.liked: Sourcing and Materials Management was directly useful for my procurement role. Management of Service Operations added a service-sector perspective I did not expect.
items.disliked: No EMI option made the payment challenging. Had to arrange semester fees upfront each time.
items.body: For operations professionals who need a formal MBA at the lowest possible cost, Galgotias is rational. The subjects are relevant, the degree is NAAC A+ accredited, and the fee is under Rs 90,000. Do not expect premium perks, strong alumni network, or recruiter access. Set expectations based on the price.

faqs.question: Does Galgotias Online MBA Operations include Six Sigma certification?
faqs.answer: No. The TQM subject covers Six Sigma DMAIC methodology as part of the curriculum, but Galgotias does not bundle a separate Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt certification. You can pursue Six Sigma certification independently.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for manufacturing careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. The curriculum covers Sourcing and Materials Management, SCM and Logistics, TQM, and Project Management, which are directly applicable to manufacturing operations, production planning, and quality management roles.

faqs.question: Does Galgotias offer project management certification with this MBA?
faqs.answer: No. The Project Management subject covers WBS, Gantt, CPM/PERT, and project risk management as academic content. Galgotias does not bundle PMP, PRINCE2, or any external project management certification.

faqs.question: How does Galgotias Operations MBA compare to LPU?
faqs.answer: LPU Online MBA Operations costs Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard, with zero-cost EMI, Coursera access, and NIRF #31 ranking. LPU provides stronger career services. Galgotias at Rs 86,400 is roughly half the cost but lacks EMI, Coursera, and placement support.

faqs.question: Does Galgotias offer EMI for this programme?
faqs.answer: No. Galgotias does not offer any EMI facility or third-party EMI partner. The fee is paid directly to the university per semester.

chooseIf: Your top priority is the lowest possible fee for a NAAC A+ MBA with operations focus. At Rs 86,400, Galgotias is second only to IGNOU.
You already work in manufacturing, logistics, or service operations and need a formal MBA credential for promotion, not brand prestige or placement support.
You need a WES-recognised operations MBA for international credential evaluation at minimal cost.
consider: If Six Sigma certification or Industry 4.0 focus matters, JIIT Online MBA (Rs 1,82,000) offers AI-core modules and mandatory internship.
If you need EMI and Coursera access, LPU Online MBA Operations at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) offers 24-month zero-cost EMI with Coursera bundled.
If NIRF ranking and manufacturing brand recognition matter, Chandigarh University (NIRF #19, Rs 1,65,000) or Amity (NIRF #22, Rs 2,25,000) are stronger options.
